---
title: "LRP52: ITT analysis of word reading (W)"

date: last-modified
date-format: "YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm:ss Z"

format:
  html:
    code-fold: true
    theme: cosmo
    number-sections: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-links:
      - repo
---

::: {.callout-warning title="Warning: Preliminary model output"}

This model output is preliminary and likely to change as the model evolves and further data are received.

If the model was not fitted in "reporting" mode, the results will be more approximate than where additional sampling has been performed.

:::

```{python}
# | echo: false
import json
from pathlib import Path

import arviz as az
import pandas as pd

_here = Path(".")
with open(_here / "config.json") as f:
    config = json.load(f)
trace = az.from_netcdf(_here / "trace.nc")
tau_summary = pd.read_csv(_here / "tau_summary.csv")
```

## Overview

LRP52 is the primary intention-to-treat (ITT) analysis for the word-reading outcome
(`ewrswr`, the YARC EWR + SWR composite). The estimand is the total effect of
randomised group assignment $G$ on the post-score $W_{\text{post}}$. The RCT phase
only (time 1 $\rightarrow$ time 2) is used.

## Model

The linear predictor is

$$
\eta_i = \alpha
       + \tau\, G_i
       + \gamma_W\, \text{logit}_*(W_{\mathrm{pre},i}, N_W)
       + \sum_{k \in \{R,E,L,P,B,F,T\}} \gamma_k\, \text{logit}_*(k_{\mathrm{pre},i}, N_k),
$$

with Beta-Binomial likelihood $W_{\text{post},i} \sim \text{BetaBinomial}(N_W, \mu_i \kappa, (1-\mu_i)\kappa)$,
$\mu_i = \sigma(\eta_i)$. Priors follow the shared template in `priors.py`.

::: {.callout-note title="HSGPs on age and own baseline are disabled by default"}

An earlier specification added HSGPs $f_A(A_{\text{std},i})$ on
standardised age and $f_{W_{\mathrm{pre}}}(\cdot)$ on the logit of the
own baseline. After the 2026-04-18 sensitivity analysis (see
`notes/202604181445-lrp52-gp-sensitivity.md`) both are **off** by
default: LOO preferred the linear model and the HSGP amplitudes
created a Neal's funnel that produced ~1 % divergences with no gain
in fit. Re-enable via the `ModelSpec.extra` dict for a sensitivity
fit.

:::

![Model diagram](model_graph.svg){#fig-model .lightbox fig-align="left"}

## Shared priors

![alpha](prior_alpha.svg){width=40%}
![tau](prior_tau.svg){width=40%}
![gamma_own](prior_gamma_own.svg){width=40%}
![gamma_cross](prior_gamma_cross.svg){width=40%}
![kappa](prior_kappa.svg){width=40%}

The eta (GP amplitude) and ell (GP lengthscale) priors are loaded
for audit purposes but have no effect in the default specification —
they appear on the prior-panel output only when the corresponding
HSGP flag is re-enabled.

## Sampling diagnostics

```{python}
# | echo: false
diag = pd.read_csv(_here / "diagnostics.csv", index_col=0)
diag
```

![Trace](trace_plot.png){.lightbox}

![Energy](energy_plot.png){.lightbox}

![Pair plot](pair_plot.png){.lightbox}

![Posterior](posterior_plot.png){.lightbox}

## Posterior predictive check

![Posterior predictive](posterior_predictive_check.png){.lightbox}

## Treatment-effect summary

```{python}
# | echo: false
tau_summary
```

- `tau_logit_*`: posterior mean and $(1-\alpha)/2$, $1-(1-\alpha)/2$ quantiles of $\tau$ on the logit scale.
- `tau_prob_*`: implied marginal effect on the probability scale, evaluated at the sample-mean baseline ($\alpha$, $\gamma_{\text{own}}$ held at their posterior means). This is a point-estimate *sensitivity* summary, not a causal contrast.
- `prob_tau_pos`: $P(\tau > 0)$.

## LOO-PSIS

```{python}
# | echo: false
# | output: asis
print("```\n" + open(_here / "loo.txt").read() + "\n```")
```

## Configuration

```{python}
# | echo: false
config
```
